message:
Welcome to The Unknown Unknown, are you prepared for the challenge?

 *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin Start button
Clicked Start button
:alarm_clock: - You have 30 minutes to resolve the outage, good luck! You can type `/help` if you need any reminders.
Hey team, getting a few reports now, looks like confusion from the customers on what they ordered?
This is strange
Just seen a couple of reports of customers not receiving the right item on delivery
Hmm? that sounds strange. @roberta.roccia can you take point and start sizing this up?
Hello. Bob is this upon delivery? so something went wrong between ordering and sending?
where do they come from?
Checking reports...
Also would like to know when they were ordered
One report said the user wanted to buy 'Sunglasses' but he got 'Tank-top' in confirmation
Just double checked with shipping looks like the items that has been ordered are correct and we're sending the right item
We have currently got more than 17 complaints so far
oh I see so it's still in the actual basket, not when they have received it at home, let me check myself
The reports are spread across all regions
:star: Reproduces the incident
Do you see the issue ?
the names do not correspond to the items
Gathering more information....
Yes, thats right
Wait one customer noticed that the image is not matching up with the product name
This will actually have a major financial impact.
We'll likely see high return rates from customers, that's going to mean a lot of financial loss from shipping and refund costs.
Seems to be that users don't know whether the product they're purchasing matches the image or the description.
We could be shipping the wrong items
Please let Bez know right away
Can you explain customer impact, what's the user experience like?
Daniel and Shay did we have any deployments lately? the names of the items on web do not correspond to the items themselves
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Do we have an incidnet ?
I am going to raise a SEV1 as this is impacting all the sales
SEV1
:star: Incident record created
*Incident Number INC100089:* SEV1
Oh no, please let me know what % of sales is affected by this quickly
Here's the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps:<https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ULPUBLIC/pages/1186431049/The+Unknown+Unknown+-+Change+Request+Logs>
What's the issue ?
Hello Bez I have raised a SEV1 as we have issues with the items in the online shop
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
The names mismatch - we could be shipping wrong items
ok just to be on the safe side lets rollback all the changes.
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Hi Roberta- We will have to make some decisions now to stop orders
also I seem to remember we have some external companies dealing with front end? Tanya can you pls help?
We will rollback all changes in PROD
But, we dont see any erros at all in the system
correct. I was asked if we should stop sales
I have rolle dnac all PROD changes, we still see the issue
Asking Bez if we can stop sales, this is not my decision
Yes, please, put a proper status messages and lets halt all trades
I can take a look into our services now
Hamed S can you jump in too to help with this? Is quite critical.
:star: Escalating when required
I need everyone in here
We don't have any alerts in the system
All apps looks healthy, no errors at all
Even i don't see any issue
Bez has agreed to add a banner on web and stop sales
Hi Roberta- what do we know till now
Must be network or platform issue.
who is going to do this?
The only option is to bring teh front end down
And put a static message
are we sure is the only option? Do we have a maintenance site banner or something?
Yes, bringing the front end down and put a maintenance message
OK discussing this in the incident channel, thanks for confirming
Lets do it ASAP
ok so please lets do this. Also get a list of all the customers who have purchased the wrong items as they will need to be refunded
I will bring down the frontend down now
I need to know who deals with the items and what appears on the website
yes Hamed is doing it
Ok the website is now down
@roberta.roccia How does it look now?
just checked and it's fine, the maintenance banner is on
Did we check if everythigns okay from Platform side?
:star: Attempt to replicate the issue
this is done
thanks
Daniel you said there are no errors but can you run some debugging?
I did, this may be a platform issue
i dont see any signals in apps side
I can see lots of errors. Something must be wrong, do do not ouput the correct info. Where do we take that from?
The issue could be anywhere, we should look at the configuration mapping between product images and names.
@Daniel do you know which application holds this data?
yes please lets do that
@Tanya ? can you check if this is stored in a database or Redis cache?
@roberta.roccia if there are no visible errors, this might be a configuration issue; the above details may help narrow it down.
Tanya I had called you before
Can we refresh the cache or something?
The configuration is stored in the Product Catalogue Service. I pushed a change in UAT yesterday that updates the Redis cache with the latest product names.
@Hamed yes, this information is stored in Redis cache and the cache was refreshed earlier today.
Checking
so what have we checked until now: rolled back all deployments
now checking redis cache
What's the latest ?
are we sure we pushed the correct details to redis? Maybe something was wrong in the UAT deployment?
:star: Reviewed changes
Do we have an ETA ? this is impacting us financially
we're checking the cache and deployments made in UAT yesterday
I can check the database and provide a Redis key dump for product:<id>:name keys
'Redis key dump' means extracting the stored data for specific Redis keys like 'product:<id>:name' so you can see what values are currently saved in the Redis database.
not yet unfortunately,
Ok here is what i found

```bash
redis-cli get product:3:name 'Sunglasses'```
You are right @Roberta - we need to check that
But product_id: 3 is actually 'mug'
It is, looks like my script has run in PROD and updated the wrong names for each product in DB
Oh no, that not good! Shall we go with a rollback?
ok so my suggestion is to rollback the UAT deployment, refresh the cache
I think this is cache issue we should clear the cache and restart Product catalogue Service, that will make fresh calls to DB and the cache will be refreshed
No, Dan's query has updated the wrong names in DB. The data is wrong in DB therfore we will have to rehydrate the canonical database with the correct product Id names .
@Tanya, i think you are complicating an easy solution. A restart should fix this
It your call @roberta.roccia
We have a major stake holders call no w- I cannot go there saying my website's down
lets rollback and then refresh
Dowe have all the resource to resolve this issue
we're rolling back
Rollign back now
Thank you, i will clear cache and bounce the servers
Hamed S what do you suggest
I think we should have gone with Tanya
I see the DB itself is updated with wrong names
Oh no, the data still appears to be wrong. I believe Tanya might be right
Tanya can you do your change on the side?
We will have to follow below steps to Rehydrate the db.
'Rehydrate the db' means restoring or repopulating the main database with accurate data from a reliable source or backup.
1. **Flush** corrupted product-name keys

```bash
redis-cli --scan --pattern 'product:*:name'| xargs redis-cli del```
who can take this?
2. **Rehydrate** product name cache from canonical DB
3. **Restart** frontend/product-catalog-service** to refresh in memory cache
Is it done
4. **Verify** product listings display correct names
Have we fixed the issue
can you do all steps or we need to allocate?
I can perform Step 1 and 2. Dan or Shay has to perform step 3
@roberta.roccia please verify that all steps have been completed
:star: Manage actions and timings
:star: Form an effective investigation team
Are we good to proceed @roberta.roccia?
didn't work, we need to repopularte the DB
pls do
Thanks for go ahead, implementing step 1 now
OK Daniel pls go ahead
Oh noo
Step 1 is now complete
Do we have a plan ?
Moving on to step 2
yes we do, we're going through the steps
Step 2 complete
lets proceed with the next 2 steps
Starting step 3.....
OK
:star: Take command
:bulb: Regularly report on outstanding actions can keep people on track
all steps almost completed
Step 3 is complete
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
The data looks correct @roberta.roccia, Can you please verify?
can you pls remove the maintenance site?
I have brought up the front end parallelly
Yes, thats done
all working now
this is fixed
we have removed the banner and all names match the items now
thank you all
@Reoberta- what do we do with the orders placed when we had wrong mapping
if they have been completed I would refund them
There are too many orders which were placed during that time
asked to refund all orders with the wrong items
Lets cancel all pending orders and refund 10$
and drop an apology note
can we refresh thos orders and see if they pick up the correct item?
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
I did check - the issue we are not sure what did the clients wanted to plac eorder
Bez says cancel all pending items and refund with $10 apology
Its better we cancel all orders
Noted thanks
yes pls
Looks like we are back to business
Well done everyone

Thank you for playing, We recommend that you take 5 minutes to go and get a drink or stretch your legs.
Once you are back we ask that you complete this retrospective and feedback form, just hit I am ready when you want to start. I am ready button
yes sales have picked up
Clicked Start_feedback button
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
yeah, thanks Roberta
Submission successful
